back_translation: Then Eliphaz the Temanite answered:
'Can a man be of use to God? Can even a wise man benefit Him?
Does it please the Almighty that you are righteous? Does He gain anything if your ways are blameless?
Is it because of your reverence that He rebukes you and enters into judgment with you?
Is not your wickedness great, and are not your iniquities endless?
For you demanded security from your brothers for no reason, and stripped the clothing from the naked.
You gave no water to the weary and withheld food from the hungry,
while the land belonged to the powerful, and the man of honor lived on it.
You sent widows away empty-handed, and the strength of the fatherless was crushed.
Therefore snares surround you, and sudden terror frightens you,
so dark that you cannot see, and a flood of water covers you.
Is not God high as the heavens? Look at the highest stars, how lofty they are!
Yet you say, 'What does God know? Can He judge through thick darkness?
Thick clouds veil Him so He cannot see us, as He walks across the vault of heaven.'
Will you keep to the ancient path that wicked men have trodden?
They were snatched away before their time, and their foundations were swept away by a flood.
They said to God, 'Depart from us! What can the Almighty do to us?'
Yet it was He who filled their houses with good things; so I keep far from the counsel of the wicked.
The righteous see it and rejoice, and the innocent mock them:
'Surely our foes are destroyed, and fire has consumed what was left of them.'
Reconcile now with Him and be at peace; then good will come to you.
Receive instruction from His mouth, and store up His words in your heart.
If you return to the Almighty, you will be restored; if you remove injustice from your tents,
and lay your gold in the dust, the gold of Ophir among the stones of the streams,
then the Almighty will be your gold and the finest silver for you.
Surely then you will delight in the Almighty and lift up your face to God.
You will pray to Him, and He will hear you, and you will fulfill your vows.
What you decide will be accomplished, and light will shine on your ways.
When people are brought low and you say, 'Lift them up!', then He will save the humble.
He will deliver even one who is not innocent; he will be rescued through the cleanness of your hands.'
